channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Court reserves judgment in Olawepo-Hashim’s Accord Party presidential candidacy suit
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Federal High Court reserves judgment in Gbenga Olawepo-Hashim’s suit against Accord Party, seeking recognition as its 2027 presidential candidate.
Read More: https://punchng.com/court-reserves-judgment-in-olawepo-hashims-accord-party-presidential-candidacy-suit/

item.title: NPC unveils online birth registration platform
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The National Population Commission (NPC) has launched an online birth registration platform, VitalReg, allowing Nigerians to register births and deaths dig
Read More: https://punchng.com/npc-unveils-online-birth-registration-platform/

item.title: Man United's Ugarte undergoes knee surgery
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Manuel Ugarte has undergone surgery after suffering a knee injury at the World Cup, Manchester United have confirmed.

item.title: Greenwood signs with Fenerbahçe from Marseille
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Former Manchester United forward Mason Greenwood on Wednesday signed for Fenerbahçe to join his third club in three years.

item.title: Lewa on Fire: No Euro club would do after Barça
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: New Chicago Fire signing Robert Lewandowski said he couldn't imagine playing for another European club after his four-season stint with Barcelona, and that explains in part his decision to move to MLS.

item.title: Mbappé critical of tactics in loss by 'sloppy' Fra...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Kylian Mbappé seemed to question Didier Deschamps' approach and tactics after France's 2-0 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup semifinal Tuesday.

item.title: England weighing man-marking plan to stop Messi
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: England coach Thomas Tuchel said he has considered man-marking Lionel Messi in Wednesday's World Cup semifinal against Argentina.

item.title: Scaloni: Argentina 'not as bad' as some people say...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Argentina manager Lionel Scaloni shut down criticism over the way his team has played throughout the FIFA World Cup ahead of the semifinal against England on Wednesday.

item.title: Spain's Yamal: 'New York, we're coming for you'
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Lamine Yamal is looking forward to take New York by storm after helping Spain reach the World Cup final with a 2-0 win against France.

item.title: Man United sign Villa's Tielemans in $47M deal
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Manchester United have announced the signing of midfielder Youri Tielemans from Aston Villa.

item.title: 📈 World Cup stock watch: Who has impressed?
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The FIFA World Cup is into the semifinals, but which players have impressed and which have struggled during both tournaments?

item.title: Oyo abduction: APM defends Makinde’s call for UN probe
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The Allied People’s Movement defends Governor Makinde’s call for a UN probe into the Oyo abduction, stating it will uncover truth and combat terrorism.
Read More: https://punchng.com/oyo-abduction-apm-defends-makindes-call-for-un-probe/

item.title: Reps demand breakdown of ₦34tn Customs waivers, query revenue reporting
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The House of Reps queries the Nigeria Customs Service over a ₦34tn import duty waiver breakdown and inconsistencies in revenue reporting. Get details here.
Read More: https://punchng.com/reps-demand-breakdown-of-%e2%82%a634tn-customs-waivers-query-revenue-reporting/

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Again, Senate rejects probe request

item.description:
The Senate has again rejected a request to probe the PFIPC scandal, citing ongoing court cases and an ICPC investigation into the controversial N1.3bn allo
Read More: https://punchng.com/pfipc-scandal-again-senate-rejects-probe-request/

item.title: USMNT's World Cup over, Pochettino's contract done...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: If Mauricio Pochettino leaves the USMNT, the question is who could replace him. Well, here are 11 coaches who could be in U.S. Soccer's hiring pool.

item.title: Zambia’s Scott, once Africa’s only white leader, dies at 82
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Zambia’s former Vice President Guy Scott, who briefly served as Africa’s only white head of state, has died at 82. Learn more about Guy Scott’s legacy.
Read More: https://punchng.com/zambias-scott-once-africas-only-white-leader-dies-at-82/

item.title: FG, IFAD equip 1,918 Nasarawa farmers with inputs, tricycles
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FG and IFAD equip 1,918 Nasarawa farmers with improved farm inputs and tricycles to boost rice and cassava production, enhancing livelihoods.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-ifad-equip-1918-nasarawa-farmers-with-inputs-tricycles/

item.title: Police rescue abducted herder, recover N2.2m ransom in Osun
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Osun State Police Command rescued a 50-year-old abducted herder, recovering N2.22 million ransom and arresting a suspected kidnapper.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-rescue-abducted-herder-recover-n2-2m-ransom-in-osun/

item.description:
A 75-year-old man, Promise Chukwumatti, has been sentenced to 10 years imprisonment for defiling an 11-year-old girl in Rivers State. The court also ordere
Read More: https://punchng.com/75-year-old-man-bags-10-years-imprisonment-for-defiling-minor-in-rivers/

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Everyone thinks they can fix the USMNT, but maybe it is making progress and luck just hasn't been on its side.

item.title: CBN Faces Mixed Inflation Signals Ahead MPC Meeting

item.description: Headline Inflation Falls To 15.91% As Monthly Food Index Rises The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) heads into its next Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting with mixed inflation signals after the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) reported a marginal decline in headline inflation, even as food pric

item.title: PSC Releases Names Of 50,000 Successful Police Constable Applicants
item.dc:creator: TVC News

item.title: Ex-CCT chairman Danladi Umar granted N100m bail over alleged abuse of office
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Former CCT Chairman Danladi Umar granted N100 million bail by FCT High Court over abuse of office and remains in custody pending fulfilment. The post Ex-CCT chairman Danladi Umar granted N100m bail over alleged abuse of office appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: George Kimmitt: France’s strategic pivot to Anglophone Africa signals new chapter in continental relations
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The geopolitical landscape of Africa is undergoing a significant transformation, marked by a recalibration of alliances and a decisive push for sovereignty by nations in the Sahel. In this context, a notable shift is underway: France, historically a dominant power in its former colonies, is increasi

item.title: FCT Projects: ‘I Don’t Make Excuses, I Deliver Results’ – Tinubu To Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Tuesday disclosed that his administration has zero tolerance for excuses, stressing that it is focused on delivering results to Nigerians. Tinubu made the remark during the commissioning of the water supply network to Bwari Satellite Town of the Federal Capital Territory, FC

item.title: It’s a buyer’s market for bonds
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Bonds are out of fashion with big investors — which makes them an interesting investment opportunity for the rest of us.

item.title: No pressure on England ahead of World Cup semi-final against Argentina – Guehi

item.description: Dementia is the seventh leading cause of death and a major cause of disability and dependency among older people globally. The post Up To 45% Of Dementia Risk Can Be Prevented, Delayed — WHO appeared first on Channels Television.

Abike Dabiri-Erewa, OON, at the Commission’s headquarters in Abuja. The meeting underscored the importance

item.title: 93 percent of inmates are State offenders, half don’t need jail — Tunji-Ojo
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: ..’How we freed 4,000 inmates in one day’ By Omeiza Ajayi ABUJA: Minister of Interior, Dr Olubunmi Tunji-Ojo, has disclosed that 93 percent of inmates in Nigerian custodial facilities are state offenders, with only 7 percent held for federal offences, adding that a significant proportion

item.title: Kenya: Court rejects Rastafarian’s bid to legalise cannabis for religious purposes
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Kenya’s High Court has dismissed a petition by the Rastafari Society of Kenya seeking to legalise the use of cannabis for religious purposes, ruling that the country’s drug laws do not violate the constitutional right to freedom of religion. In a landmark judgment, Justice Bahati Mwamuye

item.title: Revamped Cricket World Cup format could see an additional India-Pakistan match
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The 14-team 2027 ODI tournament in Africa will have a Super Seven stage but no quarterfinals.

item.title: America’s oil refiners could more than triple profits as Iran war sparks massive boom
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: U.S. oil refiners are about to report a historic profit surge as the industry takes advantage of supply disruptions.

item.title: Atlanta police gear up for trouble at England vs Argentina World Cup match
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The host city will have advanced security in place as a proactive measure against any potential crowd trouble between fans.

item.title: Anambra community rejects deputy governor’s attempt to wade into land dispute
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Indigenes of Umuenu village in Umuawulu Community of Awka South Local Government Area in Anambra State have rejected attempt by the Deputy Governor of Anambra State, Dr Onyekachi Ibezim to wade into a land dispute that had already been settled by the Supreme Court. The community had held a peaceful

item.title: Hisbah Arrests Woman Over Alleged Witchcraft In Zamfara
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Zamfara State Hisbah Commission has arrested a woman, Khadija Abdullahi, over allegations of engaging in witchcraft-related activities at her residence in the Gadar Baga area of Gusau. The commission’s spokesperson, Umar Haruna Tsafe, said the arrest followed complaints from community lead

item.title: “Having Children With Different Women Is One Of Worst Decisions A Man Can Make” – Ubi Franklin
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Businessman Ubi Franklin has advised young men against having children with multiple women, describing it as one of the worst decisions a man can make. Speaking from personal experience, Ubi said while having the financial means to cater for children is important, the real challenge lies in managing

item.title: Oyo rescue operation: Senate awards ₦50m to families of fallen heroes
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Senate has approved a ₦50 million donation for the families of five Nigerians who died during the operation that rescued schoolchildren abducted in Oyo State.

item.title: WAFU B U-20 Championship: Flying Eagles to battle Ghana, Burkina Faso, Togo
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigeria’s Flying Eagles will face Ghana’s Black Satellites, Togo’s Junior Sparrow Hawks and Burkina Faso’s Young Stallions in Group B of the WAFU B U-20 Championship, DAILY POST reports. Cote d’Ivoire will host the competition from from July 26 to August 9, 2026. The draw for the biennia

Speaking on The Rest Is Football podcast alongside Joe Cole, Micah Richards, Alan Shearer and Gary Lin

item.title: World Cup-winner Stiles' death linked to heading footballs, coroner rules
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: England 1966 World Cup winner Nobby Stiles died with a brain condition caused by repeatedly heading a football, a coroner has ruled.

item.title: Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Environment Canada issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellowSmoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the worst in the world on Wednesday and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the northeast US.Environment Canada issued health

item.title: One dead, three missing after boat capsizes near Alcatraz island
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: One person has died and three others remain missing after a pontoon boat capsized near Alcatraz Island in San Francisco Bay, United States, prompting a large-scale search and rescue operation. The San Francisco Fire Department said it received a distress call at about 3:37 p.m. local time on Tuesday

item.description: A former presidential candidate of the defunct National Conscience Party (NCP), Chief Martin Onovo, has advised Osun Governor, Ademola Adeleke, to remain confident and ensure that every vote counts in the August 15 governorship election. Onovo, a member of the Coalition of United Political Parties (

item.title: Iran FM visits Qatar days after Iranian attacks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Iran said Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi travelled to Qatar on Wednesday to pay tribute to its deceased former emir, days after the Islamic republic launched salvos at the gas-rich emirate. The post Iran FM visits Qatar days after Iranian attacks appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Smart robotic glove to restore movement, touch after paralysis developed
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: “Without feedback on how firmly an object is being held, hand function remains significantly limited in everyday life,” he said. The post Smart robotic glove to restore movement, touch after paralysis developed appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Speaker of the House of Representatives, Hon. item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Dangote Petroleum Refinery & Petrochemicals has continued to shield the Nigerian market from external price shocks with affordable petroleum products despite global market disruptions. According to the latest market intelligence from S&P Global Commodity Insights, the refinery is maintaining

item.title: Oyo abduction: Senate approves N50m donation for families of slain teachers, soldiers
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday approved a N50 million donation for the families of three soldiers and two teachers who lost their lives during the operation that rescued pupils and teachers abducted in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State. Each of the five bereaved families will receive N10 million in

item.title: UN Warns Of Cracks In Global Immunisation System
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: According to the data, an estimated 13.5 million so-called zero-dose children did not receive a single shot in their first year during 2025. The post UN Warns Of Cracks In Global Immunisation System appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: FG unveils plan to reach 40 million homes with free digital television
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Federal Government has announced that its renewed Digital Switch Over (DSO) project will provide up to 100 free television channels to at least 40 million households across Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A pro-government militia said it intercepted a boat carrying ‘weapon-making equipment for the Houthis’ in Yemen.

item.title: Former Zambia Vice President Dies At 82
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Former Zambian Vice President and former Acting President Guy Scott has died at the age of 82 following an illness, the Zambian government announced on Wednesday. President Hakainde Hichilema has directed that Scott be accorded a state funeral in recognition of his service to the southern African na

item.title: The organisation called “Nigeria”, By Dipo Baruwa
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: In 1960, the agglomeration of predominantly three nations was recognised as an independent country. This country became known as Nigeria. At the outset, all seemed to be working reasonably well. Power was distributed among its constituent units, and the determination to develop rested heavily on the

item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Lagos State Government has sealed a property at No. 5, Manuwa St., Ikoyi, for allegedly discharging untreated sewage into a canal through a non-functional sewage treatment plant. item.title: Top Foldable Smartphones of H1 2026

item.description: It directed all candidates who participated in the recently concluded written examination to check their recruitment status on the official recruitment portal.

item.title: Meet the millionaires investing in nature conservation
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: In France, a handful of wealthy entrepreneurs are buying up hundreds of hectares of land with the goal of letting nature reclaim its rightful place. Among them is Rodolphe Landemaine, an artisan baker at the helm of 30 bakeries in France and Japan. He routinely opens the doors to his Normandy estate

item.title: Tinubu Congratulates NALDA Executive Secretary, Olusegun On 40th Birthday
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has congratulated the Executive Secretary of the National Agricultural Land Development Authority (NALDA), Engr. Cornelius Adebayo Olusegun, on his 40th birthday, commending his leadership and contributions to Nigeria’s agricultural transformation. In a statement issued o

item.title: 2027: Labour Party dismisses report of failure to upload candidates’ names on INEC portal
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Labour Party has stated that it has successfully uploaded the names of all its officially nominated candidates for the Presidential and National Assembly elections prior to the closure of the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC nomination portal on July 14, 2026. This statement was m

item.title: GABI Invites African Entrepreneurs to Submit Nominations for #UnstoppableAfricans 2026
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: The Global Africa Business Initiative (GABI) has opened nominations for #UnstoppableAfricans 2026, recognising entrepreneurs, innovators and business leaders creating economic opportunities across Africa. The post GABI Invites African Entrepreneurs to Submit Nominations for #UnstoppableAfricans 2026

item.title: Kogi govt steps up efforts to rescue four abducted during NECO exams
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Kogi State Government has intensified efforts to rescue four persons abducted from Government Secondary School (GSS), Olowa, in the Dekina... The post Kogi govt steps up efforts to rescue four abducted during NECO exams appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Shettima unveils $500m Niger Delta agricultural investment fund
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “Before a people raise cities, they must learn to feed them, and before a state earns the loyalty of its citizens, it must secure their daily bread,” the vice president said. The post Shettima unveils $500m Niger Delta agricultural investment fund appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Tinubu approves N3.6bn ITF scheme for tailors, targets 200,000 artisans
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Progress Godfrey ABUJA — President Bola Tinubu has approved N3.6 billion for the Industrial Training Fund (ITF) Skill-Up Artisans (SUPA) further training programme aimed at supporting tailors and strengthening Nigeria’s informal sector. The Federal Government is targeting about 200,000 artisans f

item.title: Kebbi Govt To Pay N2.1bn For Gratuities, Death Benefits To 772 Beneficiaries
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Kebbi State Governor, Comrade Nasir Idris, has approved the release of N2,110,403,965.83 for the payment of gratuities and death benefits to state and contract staff, as well as retirees from the Local Government Service and Local Government Education Authorities. In a statement issued on Wednesday,

item.title: Welfare key to judicial independence – FCT Chief Judge
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Chief Judge of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court, Justice Husseini Baba-Yusuf, has described the welfare of judicial officers as an institutional necessity rather than a privilege, saying improved living and working conditions are essential for an independent and effective judiciary

item.title: FG Targets $130bn Chocolate Market With Cocoa Processing Drive
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has announced that Nigeria will phase out the export of raw cocoa beans in favour of local processing, as part of a broader strategy to strengthen the country’s cocoa industry through industrialisation and value addition. Speaking through the Minister of Agriculture and Food Se

item.title: Morgan Stanley Destroys Bear Case Against Nebius. Proves The 35% Plunge Was a Huge Mistake
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: HOSTCON Youth Council Hails GGSL MD For Excellence In Oil Asset Protection
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The National Executive Council (NEC) and Board of Trustees (BOT) of the Host Communities Producing Oil, Gas and Pipeline Impacted States Youth Council of Nigeria (HOSTCON Youth Council) have commended Grosvenor Global Services Limited (GGSL) for its outstanding performance in the protection of Niger

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo fans behind ‘FIFA favouring Messi’ conspiracy theories — Mikel Obi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "If they won their games, which they should have done, they would not play tougher teams. Argentina did their job, they won their games, and they got into the easier group. The post World Cup 2026: Ronaldo fans behind ‘FIFA favouring Messi’ conspiracy theories — Mikel Obi appeared first

item.title: World Cup ticket race makes WAFCON tougher – Ihezuo
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Super Falcons striker Chinwendu Ihezuo has said the upcoming 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) in Morocco will be more difficult than the last edition, but she is confident Nigeria can defend the title and qualify for the 2027 FIFA Women’s World Cup.
